2604.21629.title: Promoting Simple Agents: Ensemble Methods for Event-Log Prediction
2604.21629.paper_summary: We compare lightweight automata-based models (n-grams) with neural architectures (LSTM, Transformer) for next-activity prediction in streaming event logs. Experiments on synthetic patterns and five real-world process mining datasets show that n-grams with appropriate context windows achieve comparable accuracy to neural models while requiring substantially fewer resources. Unlike windowed neural architectures, which show unstable performance patterns, n-grams provide stable and consistent accuracy. While we demonstrate that classical ensemble methods like voting improve n-gram performance, they require running many agents in parallel during inference, increasing memory consumption and latency. We propose an ensemble method, the promotion algorithm, that dynamically selects between two active models during inference, reducing overhead compared to classical voting schemes. On real-world datasets, these ensembles match or exceed the accuracy of non-windowed neural models with lower computational cost.
2604.21629.paper_summary_zh: 我們比較了基於輕量級自動機的模型（n-grams）與神經架構（LSTM、Transformer）在串流事件日誌中的下一活動預測。對合成模式和五個真實世界的流程挖掘數據集的實驗表明，具有適當上下文窗口的 n-grams 在準確性上達到了與神經模型相當的水平，同時所需資源大幅減少。與顯示不穩定性能模式的窗口神經架構不同，n-grams 提供了穩定且一致的準確性。雖然我們證明了像投票這樣的經典集成方法可以提高 n-gram 的性能，但它們在推理過程中需要並行運行多個代理，這增加了內存消耗和延遲。我們提出了一種集成方法，即推廣算法，該算法在推理過程中動態選擇兩個活動模型之間的切換，與經典投票方案相比，減少了開銷。在真實世界的數據集上，這些集成的準確性與非窗口神經模型相匹配或超過，且計算成本更低。
2604.21629.author: Benedikt Bollig et.al.
2604.21629.authors: Benedikt Bollig, Matthias Függer, Thomas Nowak, Paul Zeinaty
